feat.name: Mark of Warding
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Warder's Intuition
entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Wards and Seals
entries: You always have the {@spell Alarm|XPHB} and {@spell Kundarak Seal|BakerKanon} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Warding Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entries.caption: Mark of Warding Spells
rows: {@spell Leomund's Secret Chest|XPHB|Durbannek's Secret Chest}*, {@spell Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum|XPHB|Ghedin's Private Sanctum}*

feat.name: Mark of Scribing
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Gifted Scribe
entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill History|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Cartographer's Tools|XPHB}, or a {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Scribe's Insight
entries: You know the {@spell Sivis Sigil|BakerKanon} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Whispering Wind|BakerKanon} spell prepared and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: Choose one of the following tools: {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Cartographer's Tools|XPHB}, or {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}. If you lack proficiency with the chosen tool, you gain proficiency with it. If you already have proficiency with the chosen tool or gain it from another source in the future, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with it.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Scribing Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

feat.name: Mark of Hospitality
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Ever Hospitable
entries: When you make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Brewer's Supplies|XPHB} or {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Innkeeper's Magic
entries: You always have the {@spell Purify Food and Drink|XPHB} and {@spell Unseen Servant|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Hospitality Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entries.caption: Mark of Hospitality Spells
rows: {@spell Prayer of Healing|XPHB} ({@spell Fortifying Feast|BakerKanon}*, {@spell Rope Trick|XPHB}*
{@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB}*, {@spell Leomund's Tiny Hut|XPHB}
{@spell Aura of Purity|XPHB}, {@spell Mordenkainen's Faithful Hound|XPHB}*

feat.name: Mark of Making
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Artisan's Intuition
entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Spellsmith
entries: You know the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Making Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

feat.name: Mark of Shadow
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cunning Intuition
entries: When you make a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) or Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Shape Shadows
entries: You know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Shadow Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

feat.name: Mark of Sentinel
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Sentinel's Intuition
entries: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB} or {@skill Perception|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Guardian's Shield
entries: You always have the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Vigilant Guardian
entries: When a creature you can see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to swap places with that creature, and you are hit by the attack instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Sentinel Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

feat.name: Mark of Handling
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Wild Intuition
entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) or Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Primal Connection
entries: You always have the {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
When you reach character level 3, you can target a Beast when you cast {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB}.

entries.name: Monstrous Connections
entries: When you reach character level 3, you can target a Monstrosity when you cast {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} or {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} if the creature's Intelligence score is 3 or lower.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Handling Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

spell.name: Kundarak's Ward
entries: You invoke the power of your dragonmark to protect an area of up to 5,000 square feet of floor space. The entire area must be contiguous space within a single structure, but you don't have to ward the entire structure.
When you cast the spell, you may apply any or all of the following effects to the warded area.

items.name: Fog
items.entry: Fog fills all warded corridors (any space with a width of 10 feet or less), making them {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}.

items.name: Locks
items.entry: All doors within the warded area are sealed with {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB}.

items.name: Lost Doors
items.entry: You may choose up to 12 doors in the warded area. These are covered with illusions to make them appear to be unbroken sections of wall.

items.name: Glyph of Warding
items.entry: You may place a single {@spell Glyph of Warding|XPHB} within the warded area. This has the power of a Glyph cast using a 3rd level spell slot, and it is dispelled when Kundarak's Ward expires.

items.name: Privacy
items.entry: Creatures in the area can't be targeted by Divination spells. Sensors created by Divination spells can't appear inside the protected area or pass through the barrier at its perimeter.

items.name: Dimensional Seal
items.entry: Nothing can teleport into or out of the warded area. Planar travel is blocked within the warded area.

entries: When you cast the spell, you can enchant a set of objects-typically coins or rings-that provide protection from its effects. Anyone who is carrying one of these tokens can see through the Fog and Lost Doors effects, open the {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB|Arcane Locks}, and safely pass the {@spell Glyph of Warding|XPHB}. The tokens have no impact on the Privacy and Dimensional Seal effects, with one restriction; a creature with a token can teleport out of the warded area, but they cannot teleport into it.

{@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} can be used to target a specific point within an area protected by Kundarak's Ward. If it is successful, it negates all effects of the Ward within a 20 foot radius from that point.

spell.name: Kundarak Seal
entries: You trace a pattern that later unleashes a deadly burst of energy.

entries.name: Sigil of Vengeance
entries: You trace the pattern on your own skin and set the type of damage it will inflict-Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. When a creature within 60 feet of you inflicts damage upon you, you may use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to trigger the sigil. The creature that inflicted damage upon you must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d8} damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The sigil remains in place until it is triggered or until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While you can have multiple sigils prepared, you can only trigger a single sigil with a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

entries.name: Elemental Trap
entries: You trace the pattern on an object that can be opened or closed (a door, a book, a chest) and set the type of damage it will inflict-Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. When someone other than you opens or closes the object the trap detonates; the triggering creature must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d8} damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The warded item is not harmed by this effect. When you cast the spell, you can set it so it will not detonate if the triggering creature either says a password while interacting with the warded object or if they possess an item you designate as a key; you must have this key item in your possession when you cast the spell. The trap remains in place until it detonates.

entries.name: Using A Higher Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@scaledice 3d8|1-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Sivis Sigil
entries: You touch an object, surface, or creature and leave a magical mark. When you cast this spell, choose one of the following effects.

entries.name: Arcane Mark
entries: You place a magical mark on the target. This symbol can be visible or invisible; an invisible mark will be revealed by {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} or any effect that allows detection of invisible objects. Sivis arcane marks are unique to each caster, but they involve blue and purple lines and often incorporate the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}. If placed on an inanimate object or surface, the mark is permanent. If placed on a living creature, the mark can be removed with {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB}; otherwise it fades in about a month.

entries.name: Explosive Rune
entries: You trace a symbol that dissolves in a deadly surge of power. Make a melee spell attack against a target within reach. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d10} damage of a type you choose: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Psychic, or Thunder.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage of Explosive Rune increases by {@dice 1d10} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d10}), 11 ({@dice 3d10}), and 17 ({@dice 4d10}).

spell.name: Whispering Wind
entries: You send a short message or sound to a location that you designate within range. You must be familiar with the target destination, and the whispering wind must have a path to the location; it cannot pass through airtight barriers. The whispering wind is silent, invisible, and intangible until it reaches its destination. It then delivers the message or sound. Note that this targets a location, not an individual; the message is delivered regardless of whether anyone is present to hear it. The whispering wind then dissipates.

The spell can deliver a message of up to 25 words. You may replace five words in the message with a short sound, such as a sharp whistle; if you wish, you could deliver a message of five distinct sounds. You must speak the message or produce the sound you wish to send as part of casting the spell, and the wind reproduces it as spoken. When you cast the spell, you decide on the speed of the wind; it can move as slowly as 1 mile per hour or as quickly as 10 miles per hour.

spell.name: Santor's Swift Scribing

components.m: {@item Lyrriman Quill|BakerKanon}, Parchment, & {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}

entries: You scribe a number of {@i Swift Scrolls}, channeling your own arcane energy into the words. You have four charges to distribute when casting this spell. You may expend one charge to create a {@i Swift Scroll} containing a {@filter 1st level spell or cantrip from the Wizard spell list|spells|level=0;1|class=wizard}. You can expend two charges to create a {@i Swift Scroll} holding a {@filter 2nd level spell from the Wizard spell list|spells|level=2|class=wizard}. Any charges you don't use immediately upon casting the spell are lost.
{@i Swift Scrolls} are identical to Spell Scrolls except as noted below.

items: Anyone can read a {@i Swift Scroll} and cast the spell, even if they aren't spellcasters. Casting the spell in this way requires the spell's normal casting time.
When the spell is cast, it uses your saving throw DC and attack bonus.
Spells on {@i Swift Scrolls} can't be copied into spellbooks.
A {@i Swift Scroll} crumbles to dust after 24 hours.

entries: A {@i Swift Scroll} bears the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon} and uses shimmering purple-blue ink. Anyone who's had any experience with House Sivis will be aware of the temporary nature of a {@i Swift Scroll}. Once you've cast Santor's Swift Scribing, you can't cast it again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

spell.name: Ghallanda's Hospitable Hall
entries: You conjure a shimmering door in range that lasts for the duration. The door leads to an extradimensional dwelling and is 5 feet wide and 10 feet tall. This duplicates the effects of {@spell Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion|XPHB}, except as noted below:

items: When you cast the spell, you may designate specific creatures that can pass through the door as long as it remains open. Alternatively, you can leave it accessible to the public, in which case any creature can pass through the door while it is open. You can open or close the door (no action required) if you are within 30 feet of it. While closed, the door is imperceptible.
You can create any floor plan you like, but it can't exceed 30 contiguous 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes}.
A staff of 15 near-transparent servants attends all who enter, with capabilities as described in {@spell Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion|XPHB}.

The Hall can produce sufficient food to serve a three course meal for up to 30 people. It can do this up to three times within a 24 hour period, but at least four hours must pass between each meal. When the Hall prepares food, you must make a Wisdom ({@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}) check to determine the quality of the food; a result of 10 is palatable, 15 is excellent, and 20 or higher is remarkable. If you're creating a meal you've never tasted before you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this check; if it's a meal you've made frequently you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check.

spell.name: Mighty Morsel
entries: Ten pellets that provide the sequential flavors of a three course meal appear in your hand and are infused with magic for the duration. A creature can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to eat one pellet. Eating a pellet restores 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, and the pellet provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day.

You must make a Wisdom ({@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}) check to determine the quality of the food; a result of 10 is palatable, 15 is excellent, and 20 or higher is remarkable. If you're creating a meal you've never tasted before you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this check; if it's a meal you've made frequently you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check.
Uneaten pellets disappear when the spell ends.

spell.name: Fortifying Feast
entries: Conjure a small meal for up to five creatures of your choice who remain within range for the spell's entire casting and consume the meal gain the benefits of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} and also regain {@dice 2d8} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. A creature can't be affected by this spell again until that creature finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You must make a Wisdom ({@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}) check to determine the quality of the food; a result of 10 is palatable, 15 is excellent, and 20 or higher is remarkable. If you're creating a meal you've never tasted before you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this check; if it's a meal you've made frequently you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The healing increases by {@scaledice 2d8|2-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 2.

item.name: Adamant Chest
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon}}

The Adamant Chest is a Tiny model of chest forged from adamantine and set with a sliver of Siberys dragonshard. If someone uses the Adamant Chest as a material component when casting {@spell Leomund's Secret Chest|XPHB|Durbannek's Secret Chest} with the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon}, they can summon a 3' x 2' x 2' chest from the Ethereal Plane. This chest has the durability of adamantine and is sealed with an {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB}; the Adamant Chest serves as the key. Casting the spell allows the bearer of the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon} to summon this chest or to send it back to the Ethereal Plane, but the chest is bound to the Adamant Chest, not to a specific caster; anyone with the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon} who gains possession of the Adamant Chest can use it to access the associated chest. There is no risk of the spell expiring and the chest being lost in the Ethereal Plane, and an heir can cast the spell with a different material component to summon their own personal chest.

item.name: Keycharm
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon}}

A Keycharm is a simple amulet, typically worn around the neck or kept in a pocket. When someone with the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon} casts {@spell Alarm|XPHB}, {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB}, {@spell Glyph of Warding|XPHB}, {@spell Kundarak's Ward|BakerKanon} or the Elemental Trap function of {@spell Kundarak Seal|BakerKanon}, they can choose to link the spell to a Keycharm in their possession. If they do this, whoever carries the Keycharm is considered to be the caster of the spell. They are aware of the location of the spell effect and can choose to end the spell or bypass its effects. Most of these spells give the caster an option to set a password or to create objects that serve as keys; the Keycharm takes this a step further, allowing the caster to give full ownership of the spell to the bearer of the Keycharm.

item.name: Locksmith's Ring
item.reqAttune: by a Creature with the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon}
entries: The Locksmith's Ring is a large iron ring with a single embedded Siberys dragonshard; it's often used as a keyring, but there is no actual need for it to carry other keys. While attuned to the Locksmith's Ring, a creature with the {@feat Mark of Warding|BakerKanon} can cast {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB} or {@spell Knock|XPHB} as a ritual; however, doing so requires 100 gp worth of refined Eberron dragonshards, which are consumed by the ritual.

item.name: Calculating Lenses
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}}

These spectacles allow a Sivis heir to apply the principles of {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} to numbers as well as words. While wearing these glasses, a creature with the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon} can take a magic item to perfectly perform basic mathematical operations involving numbers they can see. The {@i Calculating Lenses} can hold a small range of numbers on the surface of the crystal lenses, allowing the wearer to retain the results of calculations until they can be transferred to another surface.

item.name: Lyrriman Quill
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}}

If you possess the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}, you can use this pen to write on any surface; it draws vivid, purple-blue lines reminiscent of a Dragonmark. You decide whether the writing is visible or invisible, but any creature with the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon} can always see the writing. Any creature with the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon} can use a magic action to touch invisible writing created with a Lyrriman Quill and render it visible. If you use the quill to write on a living creature, the writing fades after 7 days.
If you use a Lyrriman Quill while creating a {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG}, the time required to create the Scroll is cut in half.

item.name: Listening Stone
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}}

A {@i Listening Stone} appears to be a standard spellshard. However, when activated by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}, it records conversations as words on the shard. When activating the shard, the user decides whether it will record all speech that originates within 10 feet of the stone, or only the speech of the creature holding the stone.

item.name: Sivis Slate
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}}

A {@i Sivis Slate} is a sheet of metal. If a creature uses a {@item Lyrriman Quill|BakerKanon} or a {@item Scribe's Pen|ERLW} to write on the {@i Slate} and then places the {@i Slate} on a sheet of paper or similar material, they can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to duplicate the contents of the {@i Slate} on the page below. They can also use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to clear the current contents of the {@i Slate}.

item.name: Wordbinder
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Scribing|BakerKanon}}

A {@i Wordbinder} is a pillar of metal and engraved wood. When a {@item Sivis Slate|BakerKanon} is placed on the {@i Wordbinder}, it can be used to transfer the contents to multiple pages simultaneously; this is the basis of Sivis printing.

item.name: Bag of Bounty
entries: This leather bag has three charges. A creature with the {@feat Mark of Hospitality|BakerKanon} can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to open the bag and cast {@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB}, expending one charge in the process. When using the Bag, a creature can make a Wisdom ({@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}) check to determine the quality of the food; they don't suffer the {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} that normally applies when casting {@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB} with the {@feat Mark of Hospitality|BakerKanon}. The Bag of Bounty recovers {@dice 1d3} charges at dawn.

item.name: Hosteler's Anchor
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Hospitality|BakerKanon}
entries: The Hosteler's Anchor is a stone brick engraved with the {@feat Mark of Hospitality|BakerKanon}. If the anchor is placed in an extradimensional space created by the creature attuned to Anchor—such as a {@spell Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion|XPHB|Magnificent Mansion}, {@spell Ghallanda's Hospitable Hall|BakerKanon|Hospitable Hall}, or {@spell Rope Trick|XPHB}—it can help maintain the space in the absence of the caster. When the duration of the spell expires, the caster can immediately refresh the spell through the Anchor. This requires the creator to expend the appropriate spell slot or charge from a magic item, but it requires no action on the part of the creator. The creator can even set this effect to occur even if the creator is {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}, although they must have the appropriate spell slot or item charge available to be spent. A creature who possesses both a Hosteler's Anchor and a {@item Manor Key|ExploringEberron24} can attune to both items using a single attunement slot.

item.name: Innkeeper's Gloves
entries: If you possess the {@feat Mark of Hospitality|BakerKanon}, you may use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} while wearing these gloves.

item.name: +1 Onatar's Gift
item.reqAttune: by an artificer & a Creature with the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}
entries: This simple screwdriver can transform into a variety of tools; as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch the item and transform it into any type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB|Artisan's Tool} of your choice. Whatever form the tool takes, you are proficient with it.
While holding this tool, you gain a +1 bonus to the spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your Artificer spells.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can focus on the tool to channel your creative forces. Choose a cantrip that you don't know from any class list. For 8 hours, you can cast that cantrip, and it counts as an Artificer cantrip for you. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: +2 Onatar's Gift
item.reqAttune: by an artificer & a Creature with the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}
entries: This simple screwdriver can transform into a variety of tools; as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch the item and transform it into any type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB|Artisan's Tool} of your choice. Whatever form the tool takes, you are proficient with it.
While holding this tool, you gain a +2 bonus to the spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your Artificer spells.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can focus on the tool to channel your creative forces. Choose a cantrip that you don't know from any class list. For 8 hours, you can cast that cantrip, and it counts as an Artificer cantrip for you. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: +3 Onatar's Gift
item.reqAttune: by an artificer & a Creature with the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}
entries: This simple screwdriver can transform into a variety of tools; as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch the item and transform it into any type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB|Artisan's Tool} of your choice. Whatever form the tool takes, you are proficient with it.
While holding this tool, you gain a +3 bonus to the spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your Artificer spells.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can focus on the tool to channel your creative forces. Choose a cantrip that you don't know from any class list. For 8 hours, you can cast that cantrip, and it counts as an Artificer cantrip for you. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Merrix's Instant Fortress
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can place this 1-inch adamantine statuette on the ground and, using a command word, cause it to grow rapidly into a square adamantine tower. Repeating the command word causes the tower to revert to statuette form, which works only if the tower is empty. Each creature in the area where the tower appears is pushed to an unoccupied space outside but next to the tower. Objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried are also pushed clear of the tower.

The tower is 20 feet on a side and 30 feet high, with arrow slits on all sides and a battlement atop it. Its interior is divided into two floors, with a ladder, staircase, or ramp (your choice) connecting them. This ladder, staircase, or ramp ends at a trapdoor leading to the roof. When created, the tower has a single door at ground level on the side facing you. The door opens only at your command, which you can issue as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. It is immune to the {@spell Knock|XPHB} spell and similar magic.

{@action Magic|XPHB} prevents the tower from being tipped over. The roof, the door, and the walls each have AC 20; HP 100; {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage except that which is dealt by siege equipment; and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all other damage. Shrinking the tower back down to statuette form doesn't repair damage to the tower. Only a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell can repair the tower (this use of the spell counts as replicating a spell of level 8 or lower). Each casting of Wish causes the tower to regain all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
The Cannith model was created by the same Artificer who developed the {@creature Warforged Titan|ERLW} (the grandfather of the current Merrix d'Cannith).

item.name: Apparatus of Cannith
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

This item first appears to be a sealed iron barrel weighing 500 pounds. The barrel has a hidden catch, which can be found with a successful {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. Releasing the catch unlocks a hatch at one end of the barrel, allowing two Medium or smaller creatures to crawl inside. Ten levers are set in a row at the far end, each in a neutral position, able to move up or down. When certain levers are used, the apparatus transforms to resemble a giant lobster.

The Apparatus of Cannith is a Large object with the following statistics: {@b AC} 20; {@b HP} 200; {@b Speed} 30 ft., Swim 30 ft. (or 0 ft. for both if the legs aren't extended); {@b Immunity} to Poison and Psychic damage.

To be used as a vehicle, the apparatus requires one pilot. While the apparatus's hatch is closed, the compartment is airtight and watertight. The compartment holds enough air for 10 hours of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
The apparatus floats on water. It can also go underwater to a depth of 900 feet. Below that, the vehicle takes {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning damage each minute from pressure.

A creature in the compartment can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to move as many as two of the apparatus's levers up or down. After each use, a lever goes back to its neutral position. Each lever, from left to right, functions as shown in the Apparatus of Cannith Levers table.

entries.caption: Apparatus of Cannith Levers
rows: Legs extend, allowing the apparatus to walk and swim.
Legs retract, reducing the apparatus's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} to 0 and making it unable to benefit from bonuses to speed.
Side window shutters open (two per side).
Side window shutters close (two per side).
Two claws extend from the front side of the apparatus.
Each extended claw makes the following melee attack: {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@i Hit:} 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage.
Each extended claw makes the following melee attack: {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@i Hit:} The target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}).
The apparatus walks or swims forward provided its legs are extended.
The apparatus walks or swims backward provided its legs are extended.
The apparatus turns 90 degrees counterclockwise provided its legs are extended.
The apparatus turns 90 degrees clockwise provided its legs are extended.
Eyelike fixtures emit {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.
The apparatus sinks up to 20 feet if it's in liquid.
The apparatus rises up to 20 feet if it's in liquid.
The rear hatch unseals and opens.
The rear hatch closes and seals.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Bronze Griffon
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Bronze Griffon|XDMG}}
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

A {@item Talin's Compact Construct|BakerKanon} is an articulated metal model small enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to throw the compact construct to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself, the compact construct becomes a living creature specified in the compact construct's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the compact construct doesn't become a creature.

The creature is {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each compact construct. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its compact construct form. It reverts to a compact construct early if its creature form drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or if you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while touching the creature to make it revert to compact construct form. When the creature becomes a compact construct again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

entries.name: Bronze Griffon (Uncommon)
entries: This bronze compact construct is of a griffon rampant. It can become a {@creature Griffon|XMM} for up to 6 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 5 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Ebony Fly
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ebony Fly|XDMG}}
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

entries.name: Ebony Fly (Uncommon)
entries: This ebony ompact construct, carved in the likeness of a horsefly, can become a {@creature Giant Fly|XDMG} for up to 12 hours and can be ridden as a mount. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Golden Lions
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Golden Lions|XDMG}}

entries.name: Golden Lions (Uncommon)
entries: These gold compact constructs of lions are always created in pairs. You can use one compact construct or both simultaneously. Each can become a {@creature Lion|XMM} for up to 1 hour. Once a lion has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Ivory Goats

entries.name: Ivory Goats (Uncommon)
entries: These ivory compact constructs of goats are always created in sets of three. Each goat looks unique and functions differently from the others. Their properties are as follows:

items.name: Goat of Terror
entries: This compact construct can become a {@creature Giant Goat|XMM} for up to 3 hours. The goat can't attack, but you can (harmlessly) remove its horns and use them as weapons. One horn becomes a {@item +1 Lance|XDMG}, and the other becomes a {@item +2 Longsword|XDMG}. Removing a horn requires a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, and the weapons disappear and the horns return when the goat reverts to compact construct form. While you ride the goat, any {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature that starts its turn within a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the goat must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute, until you are no longer riding the goat, or until the goat reverts to compact construct form. The {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once it succeeds on the save, a creature is immune to this effect for the next 24 hours. Once the compact construct has been used, it can't be used again until 15 days have passed.

items.name: Goat of Traveling
entries: This compact construct can become a Large goat with the same statistics as a {@creature Riding Horse|XMM}. It has 24 charges, and each hour or portion thereof it spends in goat form costs 1 charge. While it has charges, you can use it as often as you wish. When it runs out of charges, it reverts to a compact construct and can't be used again until 7 days have passed, when it regains all expended charges.

items.name: Goat of Travail
entries: This compact construct can become a {@creature Giant Goat|XMM} for up to 3 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 30 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Marble Elephant
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Marble Elephant|XDMG}}

entries.name: Marble Elephant (Uncommon)
entries: This marble compact construct resembles a trumpeting elephant. It can become an {@creature Elephant|XMM} for up to 24 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Obsidian Steed
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Obsidian Steed|XDMG}}
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

entries.name: Obsidian Steed (Rare)
entries: This polished obsidian horse can become a {@creature Nightmare|XMM} for up to 24 hours. The nightmare fights only to defend itself. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 5 days have passed.

The compact construct has a {@chance 10} chance each time you use it to ignore your orders, including a command to revert to compact construct form. If you mount the nightmare while it is ignoring your orders, you and the nightmare are instantly transported to a random location on the plane of Hades, where the nightmare reverts to compact construct form.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Onyx Dog
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Onyx Dog|XDMG}}
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

entries.name: Onyx Dog (Uncommon)
entries: This onyx compact construct of a dog can become a {@creature Mastiff|XMM} for up to 6 hours. The mastiff has an Intelligence of 8 and can speak Common. It also has {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Serpentine Owl
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Serpentine Owl|XDMG}}
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

entries.name: Serpentine Owl (Uncommon)
entries: This serpentine compact construct of an owl can become a {@creature Giant Owl|XMM} for up to 8 hours. The owl can communicate telepathically with you at any range if you and it are on the same plane of existence. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed.

item.name: Talin's Compact Construct, Silver Raven
additionalEntries: {@note Duplicated from {@item Figurine of Wondrous Power, Silver Raven|XDMG}}
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Making|BakerKanon}}

entries.name: Silver Raven (Common)
entries: This silver compact construct of a raven can become a {@creature Raven|XMM} for up to 12 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed. While in raven form, the compact construct grants you the ability to cast {@spell Animal Messenger|XPHB} on it.

item.name: Elorrenthi Rod
item.reqAttune: by a Bard with the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}
entries: This item is a {@item Thurimbar Rod|ExploringEberron24} designed to channel the power of the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}. A creature that attempts to play the Rod without being attuned to it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take {@damage 2d4} Psychic damage.

You can play the instrument to cast one of the following spells: {@spell Color Spray|XPHB}, {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB}, {@spell Fly|XPHB}, {@spell Invisibility|XPHB}, {@spell Levitate|XPHB}, {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB}, {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}. Once the instrument has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. The spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

item.name: Scrystone
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}
entries: When a creature holding this crystal uses the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon} to cast {@spell Clairvoyance|XPHB}, the spell is enhanced in the following ways.

items: The duration is increased to one hour ({@status Concentration|XPHB}).
The maximum range of the sensor is increased to 10 miles.
The spell allows the caster to both see and hear through the sensor.

The caster may communicate telepathically with a willing creature perceived through the Scrystone, provided that they either know the creature personally or that the creature is carrying a {@item Shadow Eye|BakerKanon}.

entries: A Scrystone is a disk of glass inlaid with silver, with a small siberys dragonshard embedded in the center. Scrystones are sometimes concealed within false books or otherwise disguised so that the bearer can use the stone in a public space without drawing attention.

item.name: Shadow Eye
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}}

Shadow Eyes are designed to assist Phiarlan scrying operations. A Shadow Eye is a shadow of glass with a single hair embedded within it; Eyes are created in pairs, which are bound together by a sympathetic link. This isn't an active magical effect and a Shadow Eye doesn't register as magical to {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} or similar effects. However, someone who possesses a Shadow Eye can use {@spell Clairvoyance|XPHB} or {@spell Scrying|XPHB} (including casting these spells through an item, such as a {@item Serpentine Mirror|ExploringEberron24}) to target the Eye that's linked to the one in their possession, provided it's within range of the spell. Thus, if a Shadow Eye is planted in King Boranel's bedroom, an heir with the linked Eye can spy on the king even if they aren't personally familiar with the location.

item.name: Shadow Shaper
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}}

When a creature wearing this ring uses the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon} to cast {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB}, they may choose to increase the duration of the effect to 1 hour. However, when cast in this way, the cantrip requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

item.name: Stagemaster's Crown
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}
entries: A Stagemaster's Crown is a plain leather band embroidered with silver thread and holding a single small siberys shard; it's worn as a headband. A creature wearing a Stagemaster's Crown gains the following benefits.

items: When they cast {@spell Hallucinatory Terrain|XPHB}, they can use it on unnatural environments (such as walls and furniture). This can change the outward appearance of an object but it can't make things {@condition invisible}. So in a room with a chair and a Karrnathi banner, the chair could appear to be a throne and the banner could be changed to be a Brelish banner—but the spell can't make the chair vanish.
When they cast {@spell Seeming|XPHB}, the duration is extended to 24 hours.

item.name: Thunderous Amulet
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}}
While wearing this amulet, a creature that possesses the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon} can amplify their voice to up to three times its normal volume.

item.name: Trance Academy
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}
entries: A Trance Academy is associated with three skills or tools, set when the item is created. These can include any combination of instruments, {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}, or any of the following skills: {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}, {@skill Deception|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}, or {@skill Stealth|XPHB}. When a Shadowmarked Elf completes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} while wearing the Academy, they choose one of the skills or tools associated with it; they get {@variantrule Advantage} on checks made with that skill or tool until their next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While wearing a Trance Academy, an Elf is unaware of their surroundings while trancing during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

Trance Academies are instructional tools. When an Elf wearing the Academy necklace enters trance, their mind is drawn into the illusion of a small academy, containing an art studio, stage, and classroom. This holds an echo of one of Phiarlan's great artisans or performers. In some ways this is similar to an Aereni Spirit Idol, but the echo isn't actually sentient; it's not the spirit of the Phiarlan, simply an illusion of them. Nonetheless, the echo can guide the student (the wearer of the necklace) in the practice of their specialty skills. The stage can be set to replicate one of the echo's greatest performances, or it can be set at the direction of the student, allowing them to practice for an upcoming event.

Trance Academies are created using the {@feat Mark of Shadow|BakerKanon}, and as a result the oldest Academy is three thousand years old; there are no Trance Academies tied to the original phiarlans of Xen'drik. A Trance Academy is classified as an Uncommon item because of its relative level of power, but there are only a few dozen in existence. A Shadowmarked artisan has to work with the chosen artist for years to create their echo, and Trance Academies can't be created by an Artificer's Replicate Magic Item ability or similar effects. It's a great honor for an heir of the House to be entrusted with a Trance Academy; typically this occurs when they are chosen to inherit an heirloom performance associated with the Phiarlan that cast the echo.

item.name: Cloak of the Sentinel
item.reqAttune: by a Creature with the {@feat Mark of Sentinel|BakerKanon}
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and saving throws while you wear this cloak.

item.name: Duelist's Glove
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Sentinel|BakerKanon}
entries: These gloves are commonly issued to heirs serving in the Defender's Guild, and bear the seal of the Defenders on the back of the hand. When the wearer casts {@spell Compelled Duel|XPHB}, they gain the following benefits.

items: The target of the spell has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
You don't need {@status Concentration|XPHB} to maintain the spell.

item.name: Ring of Shared Suffering
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Sentinel|BakerKanon}
entries: These rings come in linked pairs. If you possess the {@feat Mark of Sentinel|BakerKanon}, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to form a link to the creature attuned to the other ring; from then on, whenever that creature suffers damage, they only suffer half of that damage and you take the rest. This effect continues until you end it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or until you or the other creature removes their ring. This Ring isn't limited by range, though both creatures must be on the same plane to benefit from its effects. A creature cannot be attuned to more than one Ring of Shared Suffering.

item.name: Scepter of Wild Dominion
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}
entries: While holding this rod, you have the following benefits.

items: You gain a +2 bonus to the saving throw DC of your Spells of the Mark.
You cast Spells of the Mark as if using a spell slot one level higher than the slot you actually expend.
You may cast {@spell Command|XPHB}, {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB}, and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} without using a spell slot.

item.name: Collar of the Wild Bond (Uncommon)
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}}

If you possess the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}, you may use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast {@spell Dominate Beast|XPHB} on a Beast you can see that's wearing a Collar of the Wild Bond. This doesn't use a spell slot, but the other limitations of the spell apply. The effect has a range of 60 feet, and the creature can negate the effect with a successful Wisdom saving throw; your spellcasting ability for this effect is the same one you chose for the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}. You must concentrate to maintain the effect, but as long as you are within 200 feet of the creature, you can maintain the spell indefinitely.

item.name: Collar of the Wild Bond (Rare)
entries: {@note Requires the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}}

If you possess the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}, you may use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast {@spell Dominate Beast|XPHB} on a Beast you can see that's wearing a Collar of the Wild Bond. This doesn't use a spell slot, but the other limitations of the spell apply. The effect has a range of 60 feet, and the creature can negate the effect with a successful Wisdom saving throw; your spellcasting ability for this effect is the same one you chose for the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}. You must concentrate to maintain the effect, but as long as you are within 200 feet of the creature, you can maintain the spell indefinitely.
The Rare version of the Collar can be used on Monstrosities with an Intelligence of 3 or less.

item.name: Balinor's Blessing
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}
entries: This six-foot stone pillar is engraved with the patterns of the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}. As long as a creature with the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon} is attuned to the pillar—a process that must be repeated every day—It has the following effects within a 2000 foot radius.

items: Beasts have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws. They are remarkably healthy, fertile, and resistant to mundane disease.
Beasts have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws to resist any spell effect associated with the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon}.
Any creature with the {@feat Mark of Handling|BakerKanon} has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) checks.

item.name: Hurricane Cloak
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA}
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make it billow dramatically for one minute. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to catch the wind within the cloak, lifting you just off the ground. While the cloak remains active, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 feet and can hover. You must maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} to sustain this flight, as if you were {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on a spell. The cloak keeps you aloft until you end your {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

item.name: Windwright's Anchor
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA}
entries: This amulet enhances the powers of the {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA}. When you cast {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB}, {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB}, {@spell Wind Wall|XPHB} or {@spell Conjure Elemental|XPHB}, you can use the Anchor to enhance the duration of the spell. This requires intense focus and ongoing {@status Concentration|XPHB}. While using the Anchor in this way, you are {@condition Restrained|XPHB}. In addition, you must use an action on each of your turns to maintain the effect. As long as you do so, you can maintain the spell effect indefinitely.

item.name: Raincaller's Crown
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA}
entries: When you cast {@spell Control Weather|XPHB}, you can maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell for up to 24 hours. You must be outdoors to cast the spell, but it doesn't end early if you go indoors after casting it. When you end {@status Concentration|XPHB}, the weather you have created continues for eight hours before fading.

item.name: Scepter of the Firstborn
item.reqAttune: by a creature with the {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA}
entries: This {@item Rod|XPHB} has 7 charges and can be wielded as a {@item Mace|XPHB}.

entries.name: Mighty Thunder
entries: If you cast {@spell Thunderclap|XPHB} while holding the Scepter, the damage is increased by {@dice 1d6} and the saving throw DC is increased by 2.

entries.name: Storm Unleashed
entries: While holding the Scepter, you can expend up to 3 charges to cast {@spell Lightning Bolt|XPHB} ({@variantrule Save|XPHB} DC 15) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the level 3 version of the spell. You can increase the spell's level by 1 for each additional charge you expend.

entries.name: Regaining Charges
entries: The Scepter regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

inherits.reqAttune: by a Creature with the {@feat Mark of Sentinel|BakerKanon}
entries: As long as this weapon is within your reach and you are attuned to it, you and allies within 30 feet of you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Alarm
entries: The weapon magically awakens each subject who is sleeping naturally when combat begins. This benefit doesn't wake a subject from magically induced sleep.

entries.name: Supernatural Readiness
entries: Each subject has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.
{@note Duplicated from {@item Weapon of Warning|XDMG}}

vehicle.name: Apparatus of Cannith

speed.note: (or 0 ft. for both if the legs aren't extended)

entries: This item first appears to be a sealed iron barrel weighing 500 pounds. The barrel has a hidden catch, which can be found with a successful {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. Releasing the catch unlocks a hatch at one end of the barrel, allowing two Medium or smaller creatures to crawl inside. Ten levers are set in a row at the far end, each in a neutral position, able to move up or down. When certain levers are used, the apparatus transforms to resemble a giant lobster.

To be used as a vehicle, the apparatus requires one pilot. While the apparatus's hatch is closed, the compartment is airtight and watertight. The compartment holds enough air for 10 hours of breathing, divided by the number of breathing creatures inside.
The apparatus floats on water. It can also go underwater to a depth of 900 feet. Below that, the vehicle takes {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning damage each minute from pressure.

A creature in the compartment can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to move as many as two of the apparatus's levers up or down. After each use, a lever goes back to its neutral position. Each lever, from left to right, functions as shown in the Apparatus of Cannith Levers table.

entries.caption: Apparatus of Cannith Levers
rows: Legs extend, allowing the apparatus to walk and swim.
Legs retract, reducing the apparatus's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} to 0 and making it unable to benefit from bonuses to speed.
Side window shutters open (two per side).
Side window shutters close (two per side).
Two claws extend from the front side of the apparatus.
Each extended claw makes the following melee attack: {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage.
Each extended claw makes the following melee attack: {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}The target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}).
The apparatus walks or swims forward provided its legs are extended.
The apparatus walks or swims backward provided its legs are extended.
The apparatus turns 90 degrees counterclockwise provided its legs are extended.
The apparatus turns 90 degrees clockwise provided its legs are extended.
Eyelike fixtures emit {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.
The apparatus sinks up to 20 feet if it's in liquid.
The apparatus rises up to 20 feet if it's in liquid.
The rear hatch unseals and opens.
The rear hatch closes and seals.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned how to channel magical energy through objects. See the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Artificer spells, which appear in the {@filter Artificer spell list|spells|class=Artificer (kbk)} later in the class's description.

entries.name: Tools Required
entries: You produce your Artificer spells through tools. You can use {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}, or another kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}, and you must have one of those focuses in hand when you cast an Artificer spell (meaning the spell has an {@i M} component when you cast it).

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two {@filter Artificer cantrips|spells|level=0|class=Artificer (kbk)} of your choice. {@spell Acid Splash|XPHB} and {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} are recommended.
Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Artificer cantrip of your choice.
When you reach Artificer levels 10 and 14, you learn another Artificer cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Artificer Features table.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Artificer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two {@filter level 1 Artificer spells|spells|level=1|class=Artificer (kbk)}. {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} and {@spell Grease|XPHB} are recommended.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Artificer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Artificer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional {@filter Artificer spells|spells|class=Artificer} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Artificer, your list of prepared spells can include six Artificer spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.

If another Artificer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Artificer spells for you.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Artificer spells for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Artificer spells.

classFeature.name: Replicate Magic Item
entries: You have learned arcane plans that you use to make magic items.

entries.name: Kanon Change
entries: You gain access to all Magic Item Plans when you reach the appropriate level.

entries.name: Creating an Item
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one or two different magic items if you have {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} in hand. Each item is based on one of the plans you know for this feature.

If a created item requires {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you can attune yourself to it the instant you create it. If you decide to attune to the item later, you must do so using the normal process for {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}.

When you reach certain Artificer levels specified in the Magic Items column of the Artificer Features table, the number of magic items you can create at the end of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} increases. Each item you create must be based on a different plan you know.

You can't have more magic items from this feature than the number shown in the Magic Items column of the Artificer Features table for your level. If you try to exceed your maximum number of magic items for this feature, the oldest item vanishes, and then the new item appears.

entries.name: Duration
entries: A magic item created by this feature functions as the normal magic item, except its magic isn't permanent; when you die, the magic item vanishes after {@dice 1d4} days. If you replace a plan you know with a new plan, any magic item created with the replaced plan immediately vanishes.
If an item that you created with this feature is a container, such as a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, and it vanishes, its contents harmlessly appear in and around its space.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use any Wand or Weapon created by this feature as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} in lieu of using a set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}.

entries.name: Crafting More Magic Items
entries: The Replicate Magic Item feature isn't the only way for an Artificer to make magic items. The {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG|6|Crafting Magic Items} provides rules for characters—not just Artificers—who want to make magic items of all sorts. Though many different characters can make magic items, Artificers can do it faster than others; each Artificer subclass improves a character's speed at making items of a certain category.

When an Artificer crafts a magic item using the normal rules in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG|6|Crafting Magic Items}, the character gains no special connection to that item. Class features that refer to items created by your Replicate Magic Item feature don't apply to items you craft by other means.

entries.caption: Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 2+)
rows: {@filter Common magic item that isn't a Potion, a Scroll, or cursed|items|source=|type=!potion;!scroll|rarity=common|category=|miscellaneous=!cursed}*
{@item +1 Wand of the War Mage|XDMG|Wand of the War Mage +1}
{@item +1 Wraps of Unarmed Power|XDMG|Wraps of Unarmed Power, +1}
footnotes: *You can learn this option multiple times and must select a different item each time; each item selected counts as a different plan.

rows: {@filter Uncommon Wondrous Item that isn't cursed|items|source=|type=wondrous item|rarity=uncommon|category=|miscellaneous=!cursed}*

{@item +2 Wraps of Unarmed Power|XDMG|Wraps of Unarmed Power, +2}
footnotes: *You can learn this option multiple times and must select a different item each time; each item selected counts as a different plan.

footnotes: *You can learn this option multiple times and must select a different item each time; each item selected counts as a different plan.

classFeature.name: Artificer Subclass
entries: You gain an Artificer subclass of your choice. The {@subclass Alchemist|Artificer|BakerKanon|BakerKanon}, {@subclass Armorer|Artificer|BakerKanon|BakerKanon}, {@subclass Artillerist|Artificer|BakerKanon|BakerKanon}, and {@subclass Battle Smith|Artificer|BakerKanon|BakerKanon}, and {@subclass Cartographer|Artificer|BakerKanon|BakerKanon} subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Artificer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Artificer level or lower.

classFeature.name: Subclass feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Magic Item Tinker
entries: Your {@classFeature Replicate Magic Item|Artificer|BakerKanon|2|BakerKanon} feature gains the following options.

entries.name: Charge Magic Item
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and that uses charges. You expend a level 1+ spell slot and recharge the item. The number of charges the item regains is equal to the level of spell slot expended.

entries.name: Drain Magic Item
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and cause the item to vanish, converting its magical energy into a spell slot. The slot is level 1 if the item is Common or level 2 if the item is Uncommon or Rare. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Transmute Magic Item
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch one magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and transform it into a different magic item. The resulting item must be based on a magic item plan you know. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Subclass feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Magic Item Adept
entries: You can now attune to up to four magic items at once.

classFeature.name: Subclass feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

classFeature.name: Subclass feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

subclassFeature.name: Perfected Elixirs
entries: You can create perfect elixirs with potent effects. When you produce elixirs at the end of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you also create one Panacea elixir and one Vivacity elixir. These elixirs follow the same rules as your other elixirs, but they can't be produced in any other way.

entries.name: Panacea
entries: The drinker is restored to full {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and all conditions affecting them are ended. If the drinker has died within the last hour, they instead revive with 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}. This elixir can't revive a creature that has died of old age, nor does it restore any missing body parts.

entries.name: Vivacity
entries: The drinker gains the benefits of your Resilience, Boldness, and Flight elixirs for 8 hours. When gained in this way, the Flight elixir grants a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 50 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Overcharge
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can overcharge your armor to unleash a torrent of energy forming a 100-foot-long, 15-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that blasts out from you in a direction you choose. The type of energy unleashed by the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} depends on your current armor model: Dreadnaught deals Force damage, Guardian deals Thunder damage, and Infiltrator deals Lightning damage. Each creature in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 30d6} damage of the appropriate type on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Infused Cannon
entries: You can infuse your cannons with eldritch energy, empowering them to produce more powerful effects. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cause one or both of your cannons to produce one of the effects listed below. Multiple cannons can use the same option or different ones.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required).

entries.name: Dragonflame
entries: The cannon exhales fire in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in that area makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 5d8} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Flammable objects in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} that aren't being worn or carried start burning.

entries.name: Barrage
entries: The cannon lobs a ball of concentrated energy at a point you choose within 120 feet of it. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point makes a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 5d8} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A nonmagical object that isn't being worn or carried also takes the damage if it's in the target area, and this damage ignores the damage threshold of objects.

entries.name: Restoration
entries: The cannon emits a wave of curative energy, targeting itself and each creature of your choice within 10 feet of it. Each target regains 25 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and is cured of the Blind, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, and {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} conditions.

subclassFeature.name: Steel Vanguard
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} in hand, you infuse your {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} with arcane energy, granting it the following benefits for 1 minute.

entries.name: Multiattack
entries: The {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} gains the Multiattack action, allowing it to make four Force-Empowered Rend attacks.

entries.name: Flight
entries: The {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Protective Shield
entries: The {@creature Steel Defender|EFA} gains 50 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries.name: Limitless Jolt
entries: Using Arcane Jolt does not expend uses.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a level 5 spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Travel Anywhere
entries: If you are one of the map holders for your Adventurer's Atlas, you can cast {@spell Gate|XPHB} without a Material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

When you cast the spell with this feature, the duration extends to 10 minutes and damage can't cause you to lose {@status Concentration|XPHB}. Also, you can choose to link the portal to a precise location on the same plane of existence or a different plane of existence.

subclassFeature.name: Weapon of Legend
entries: You are able to connect your spirit to your allies, making them living weapons. You gain the following benefits while wielding your Ghaal'Shaarat.

entries.name: Constant Aura
entries: Your Aura of War is always active, and you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to change its damage type.

entries.name: Retributive Aura
entries: Whenever an enemy starts its turn in your Aura of War, that creature takes damage equal to the extra damage provided by the aura.

entries.name: Empower Aura
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can increase the extra damage provided by your Aura of War to {@dice 2d6}. This increase lasts for 1 minute. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Genius
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast any spell of level 8 or lower. This takes the place of the usual casting time of the spell, and you don't need to meet any requirements to cast the spell, including costly components; the spell simply takes effect. Once you cast a spell with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
